# Extending primary event censoring

## More distributions, more often solved exactly

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
- [primarycensored](https://primarycensored.epinowcast.org/) now
  dispatches 25 delay distributions, up from 17
- Eight added for the Stan likelihood: normal, double exponential,
  Pareto, inverse and scaled-inverse chi-square, Student's t,
  uniform, von Mises
  ([#277](https://github.com/epinowcast/primarycensored/pull/277),
  [#314](https://github.com/epinowcast/primarycensored/pull/314))
- Lower truncation $L$ may now be negative or $-\infty$, so
  signed-support delays (normal, logistic, Cauchy, Gumbel) work too
  ([#311](https://github.com/epinowcast/primarycensored/pull/311))
- Exact primary-censored CDFs for gamma, log-normal and Weibull
  under a uniform primary window; everything else falls back to
  numerical integration
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
| Delay | Primary | Solution |
|---|---|---|
| Gamma | Uniform | Analytic |
| Log-normal | Uniform | Analytic |
| Weibull | Uniform | Analytic |
| Others (22) | Any | Numerical |

::: {.callout-note}
Analytic forms cut a numerical integral to a few CDF calls, so the
likelihood is faster and more stable for inference at scale.
:::
:::
::::

::: {.attribution}
[Analytic solutions
vignette](https://primarycensored.epinowcast.org/articles/analytic-solutions.html);
rewritten in CDF-direct form in
[#306](https://github.com/epinowcast/primarycensored/pull/306)
:::

## Point-source outbreaks are primary censoring

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
- A point-source outbreak: many cases share one brief, often known,
  exposure window, then onset follows after the incubation period
- That shared window is exactly a known primary-censoring interval,
  common to every case
- So the incubation-period distribution is the secondary delay, and
  [primarycensored](https://primarycensored.epinowcast.org/)
  estimates it directly from onset times
- Onsets are also reported by day, so secondary censoring and
  right-truncation come along for free
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
- Classic outbreak investigation, with censoring-aware estimation
  doing the arithmetic
- The same likelihood that fits transmission-pair serial intervals
  fits common-exposure incubation data
- Exact solutions matter most here, where the shared window is fixed
  and reused across thousands of cases
:::
::::

::: {.attribution}
Point-source outbreak: a common-source outbreak with exposure shorter
than one incubation period
([CDC, Principles of
Epidemiology](https://archive.cdc.gov/www_cdc_gov/csels/dsepd/ss1978/lesson1/section11.html))
:::
